changes.before: Not winter cold. Winter stopped at the skin. This kept going.
changes.after: Winter stopped at the skin. This kept going.

changes.before: "Minds. It isn't just throwing hunger in every direction now. It touches, tests, lets go."
The wall deserved plain speech.
changes.after: Plain speech carried best through stone.

changes.before: The palace itself was giving ground to the sword.
changes.after: The sword's cold was working deeper into the palace stone.

changes.before: Silence stretched. Not empty. Listening.
Jaina closed her eyes and gave the wall what it had earned.
changes.after: Jaina closed her eyes and said it into the stone between them.

changes.before: The truth landed harder than the verdict had.
Not laughter. Not a sigh. More like the breath a fighter lets out when a blow lands where it was meant to.
changes.after: More like the breath a fighter lets out when a blow lands where it was meant to.

changes.before: Not ordinary dark. Palace dark still had traces in it: a seam of light under a door, the pale bleed of old stone, the memory of a corridor beyond. This came in thick.
changes.after: Palace dark still had traces in it: a seam of light under a door, the pale bleed of old stone, the memory of a corridor beyond. This came in thick.

changes.before: One moment the cell held only Sylvanas and the dark. The next it held another body, and the space between them shortened as if the room itself had given up ground.
changes.after: One moment the cell held only Sylvanas and the dark. The next it held another body, and the space between them shortened under its size.

changes.before: Not hungry like the blade. Watching.
"The sword will take another hand. The roads he opened remain open. The cells you never found are still waiting. One man was useful. He was never the whole design."
changes.after: "The sword will take another hand. The roads he opened remain open. You still never found all the cells. One man was useful. He was never the whole design."

changes.before: One corner of its mouth shifted. Not a smile. Recognition of a neat hit.
One corner of its mouth shifted. Not a smile. Recognition of a neat hit.
Not from tonight alone. From the road.
Not coincidence. Not provincial habit. A tell.
Jaina shut her eyes for a moment.
Kel'Thuzad had known things too. More than she did, perhaps, about the deeper workings.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad had known things too. More than she did about the deeper workings.

changes.before: The word did what comfort could not.
There.
That was the seam.
One word. Enough.
That was enough. The sentence placed it beside the weapon's birth and gave it the tone of weather seen through a window.
Enough to begin.
Fed them truth when truth served it.
changes.after: Fed them a name here, a road there, a warning when it served.

changes.before: Cleaned the ground ahead of them.
changes.after: Cleared the next stretch of road before they reached it.
